title: The Top 10 Garbage Lads Movies and ‘Guy Ritchie’s The Covenant’
format: Sean and Chris Ryan review Guy Ritchie's two 2023 films, then trace the whole British-crime 'garbage lads' lineage from Brighton Rock to The Gentlemen.

segments.heading: Guy Ritchie's The Covenant
segments.note: the one that doesn't fit the genre

films.title: Guy Ritchie's The Covenant
films.note: Jake Gyllenhaal as a U.S. Army sergeant who returns to Afghanistan to rescue the interpreter who saved his life; both hosts liked it, though Ryan preferred the first-half war movie to the second-half rescue plot, the inverse of most reviews.

segments.heading: Guy Ritchie's zigzag career
segments.note: the 14th film from a director who 'doesn't always make what we'll be describing today'

films.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword
films.note: the film that had Ritchie on the show as a guest, in a tweed suit, rewatching Snatch on YouTube with Jason Statham on Zoom.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: Ryan calls it "completely misbegotten" and "absolutely dreadful."

films.title: The Gentlemen
films.note: "a bit of a return to form."

films.title: Wrath of Man
films.note: a Jason Statham crime movie played dead serious, with none of the usual humor.

films.title: The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare
films.note: Ritchie's next film; Fennessey guesses it's a return to "a comfortable place."

films.title: Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels
films.note: the film that revived the subgenre in the late '90s.

films.title: Snatch
films.note: Ryan calls it arguably the best film in the whole tradition; he rewatched it with subtitles on for the first time and finally understood every line.

films.title: RocknRolla
films.note: Ryan's secretly favorite Guy Ritchie movie.

films.title: Sherlock Holmes
films.note: one of a few Ritchie films the hosts read as an unofficial Bond tryout.

films.title: Swept Away
films.note: Ritchie's Madonna-starring remake of Lina Wertmüller's film — "a mystifying choice."

segments.heading: Operation Fortune: Ruse de Guerre
segments.note: a Bond tryout, mostly for Ritchie himself

films.title: Operation Fortune: Ruse de Guerre
films.note: Jason Statham, Aubrey Plaza, Josh Hartnett, Cary Elwes, and Hugh Grant as the heavy; Ryan liked it less than The Covenant, calling the whole cast down-tempo and disinterested, though he still found it more entertaining than most of what's in theaters.

films.title: Brighton Rock
films.note: Graham Greene adaptation with Richard Attenborough as Pinky; Ryan's pick for the first movie in the genre.

films.title: Victim
films.note: Basil Dearden, cited as required viewing for anyone making these films.

films.title: The Criminal
films.note: Joseph Losey, with Stanley Baker as a criminal who unwittingly robs a rival mob boss and lands in a much worse jam.

films.title: Get Carter
films.note: Mike Hodges, Michael Caine — a name check that recurs through the whole episode.

films.title: The Long Good Friday
films.note: Bob Hoskins; where Ryan says the genre "really kind of starts."

films.title: Mona Lisa
films.note: Bob Hoskins again, in what the hosts call a near-melodrama with crime around the edges.

films.title: The Offence
films.note: Sidney Lumet and Sean Connery as a cop who loses it interrogating a murder suspect.

segments.heading: The '90s edge cases
segments.note: does it need to be funny? does it need to be a lad?

films.title: A Fish Called Wanda
films.note: Charles Crichton; the hosts count it in even though it's a comedy.

films.title: Naked
films.note: Mike Leigh — David Thewlis's character counts, per Ryan, because "he is a criminal."

films.title: Shallow Grave
films.note: Danny Boyle; counts because a body and a suitcase of money sit at the center of the plot.

films.title: Trainspotting
films.note: Danny Boyle again — the hosts rule it OUT of the genre; it's about addiction, and the heist only shows up to end the movie.

films.title: Sexy Beast
films.note: Jonathan Glazer's directorial debut, Ben Kingsley and Ray Winstone; the film both hosts keep returning to as the genre's high point.

segments.heading: Layer Cake and the Matthew Vaughn wing
segments.note: Ritchie's old producing partner goes his own way

films.title: Layer Cake
films.note: Vaughn's directing debut, with Daniel Craig; got Craig cast as Bond, and Ryan argues it's aged better than the Ritchie films it's imitating.

films.title: X-Men: First Class
films.note: "arguably the best X-Men movie."

films.title: Argylle
films.note: Vaughn's forthcoming Henry Cavill spy film, described from its logline.

films.title: Croupier
films.note: Mike Hodges again, 25 years after Get Carter, plucking Clive Owen out of obscurity.

films.title: Casino Royale
films.note: Craig's actual Bond debut — "essentially Layer Cake but with better suits."

segments.heading: The wave after Ritchie and Vaughn
segments.note: Bronson, Wheatley, and the Kray brothers

films.title: Bronson
films.note: Nicolas Winding Refn, with Tom Hardy.

films.title: Down Terrace
films.note: Ben Wheatley's first feature.

films.title: Kill List
films.note: Ben Wheatley.

films.title: Harry Brown
films.note: Michael Caine, cashing in on his '70s iconography being back in vogue.

films.title: Legend
films.note: Tom Hardy playing both Kray twins.

films.title: London Boulevard
films.note: Colin Farrell; William Monahan directing, though Ryan wonders how much of his own script survived onscreen.

films.title: In Bruges
films.note: Martin McDonagh and Ralph Fiennes; Ryan calls McDonagh one of the great what-if garbage lads directors, before he moved fully into American films.

films.title: Trance
films.note: Danny Boyle — Ryan calls it mind-blowing and says it's underrated.

films.title: Filth
films.note: James McAvoy in an Irvine Welsh adaptation, playing a disgusting cop.

films.title: The Meg
films.note: Jason Statham; "certainly not a garbage lads movie" — more garbage shark.

films.title: Meg 2: The Trench
films.note: Ben Wheatley directs, a long way from where his career started.

films.title: A Field in England
films.note: Ben Wheatley's psychedelic period.

films.title: In the Earth
films.note: Wheatley's COVID-era horror movie — compelling but not exactly entertaining, per the hosts.

films.title: Free Fire
films.note: Brie Larson and Tom Hardy; Ryan calls it "definitely a garbage lads film."

films.title: Rebecca
films.note: Wheatley's Armie Hammer remake, done "quite poorly" per Ryan.

films.title: High-Rise
films.note: Tom Hiddleston, a J.G. Ballard adaptation Ryan found "pretty nifty."

films.title: King of Thieves
films.note: James Marsh; Michael Caine, Tom Courtenay, and Jim Broadbent as old guys still got it, robbing a bank with Charlie Cox.

films.title: The Hit
films.note: Stephen Frears, with Tim Roth in an early role.

films.title: Shifty
films.note: an early Riz Ahmed film.

films.title: Ill Manors
films.note: another early Riz Ahmed film.

films.title: Starred Up
films.note: Jack O'Connell.

films.title: Prick Up Your Ears
films.note: an early Gary Oldman performance.

films.title: Nil by Mouth
films.note: Oldman's directorial debut.

films.title: The Firm
films.note: Oldman as a soccer hooligan.

films.title: Face
films.note: Antonia Bird, with Robert Carlyle as a socialist turned thief.

films.title: Dom Hemingway
films.note: Jude Law fresh out of prison, reuniting with a daughter played by Emilia Clarke; "40% garbage lad, 60% reuniting with my daughter," per Ryan.

segments.heading: London, Sweeney, and the sign-off
segments.note: a hypothetical trip, plus what Steve McQueen's up to

films.title: Sweeney!
films.note: David Wickes; Ryan's pick for a special London screening, alongside The Long Good Friday and Sexy Beast.

films.title: Calm with Horses
films.note: Barry Keoghan, cited as recent proof the tradition still has life in it.

films.title: The 355
films.note: a digression — Jessica Chastain and Lupita Nyong'o, "not good."

films.title: The Old Guard
films.note: Gina Prince-Bythewood, Charlize Theron; another digression on female-led action.

films.title: Boiling Point
films.note: Stephen Graham as a chef — a pure tangent about food, not crime.

films.title: Widows
films.note: Steve McQueen; the hosts debate whether it still counts as garbage lads despite being set in Chicago.

films.title: Occupied City
films.note: McQueen's four-hour Holocaust documentary.

films.title: Blitz
films.note: McQueen's next feature, starring Saoirse Ronan and Harris Dickinson, about Londoners during the WWII bombing campaign.

King Arthur: Legend of the Sword.title: King Arthur: Legend of the Sword

Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels.title: Lock, Stock and Two Smoking Barrels
